profile.title: Profile
profile.name: Name
profile.newEmailMustDiffer: New email must be different from current email
profile.changeEmailDescription: Enter your new email address and current password. We'll send a verification code to the new email.
profile.enterCodeToConfirm: Enter it below to confirm the change.
profile.totp.securityDescription: Manage two-factor authentication for your account.
profile.totp.label: Two-factor authentication
profile.totp.scanDescription: Scan this QR code with your authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, 1Password, etc.).
profile.totp.confirmDescription: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app to verify setup.
profile.totp.backupCodesWarning: Save these backup codes in a safe place. Each code can only be used once. If you lose access to your authenticator app, you can use a backup code to sign in.
profile.totp.disableDescription: Enter your password to disable two-factor authentication. Your account will only be protected by your password.
profile.totp.regenDescription: This will invalidate your existing backup codes and generate new ones. Enter your password to continue.
logout.title: Log out?
logout.message: You'll be signed out of the Admin Console.
apiKeys.title: API Keys
apiKeys.description: API keys allow external systems to access your workspace. Keep them secret since they cannot be recovered once created.
apiKeys.limitReached: You can have a maximum of {{max}} API keys per workspace. Delete an existing key to create a new one.
apiKeys.createDescription: Choose a name to help you recognize this key later. You'll be shown the full key once after creation.
apiKeys.limitReachedCreate: API key limit reached ({{max}}). Delete an existing key to create a new one.
apiKeys.createdDescription: This is the only time you'll be shown the full API key. Copy it now and store it somewhere safe.
apiKeys.deleteDescription: This will permanently delete "{{name}}". This action cannot be undone.
apiKeys.editDescription: Change the name of this API key. The key itself will not be regenerated.
cors.title: CORS Origins
cors.description: This is for calling the ManyRows API directly from a browser (CORS). When a browser makes requests to the API, it will send Authorization: Bearer <JWT> on requests after you sign in.
cors.hint: Add the origins (scheme + host + optional port) you want to allow, like https://example.com or http://localhost:3000. No path/query.
cors.limitReached: You can have a maximum of {{max}} CORS origins per app. Delete an existing origin to add a new one.
cors.addDescription: Add an origin that's allowed to call the API from a browser. In JWT mode, the browser will send Authorization: Bearer <JWT> (and will often do a preflight OPTIONS request first). Enter a valid origin (scheme + host, optional port). No path/query.
cors.deleteDescription: This will remove "{{origin}}" from the allowlist.
cors.editDescription: Update the origin URL. Enter a valid origin (scheme + host, optional port). No path/query.
ipAllowlist.title: IP Allowlist
ipAllowlist.description: Restrict API access to specific IP addresses or CIDR ranges. When enabled, only requests from listed IPs will be allowed. If no IPs are configured, all IPs are allowed by default.
ipAllowlist.hint: Enter individual IPs (e.g., 203.0.113.50) or CIDR ranges (e.g., 10.0.0.0/8, 192.168.1.0/24).
ipAllowlist.limitReached: You can have a maximum of {{max}} IP allowlist entries per app. Delete an existing entry to add a new one.
ipAllowlist.noEntries: No IP restrictions configured. All IPs are currently allowed.
ipAllowlist.addDescription: Add an IP address or CIDR range that's allowed to access the API. Use CIDR notation for ranges (e.g., 192.168.1.0/24).
ipAllowlist.editDescription: Update the IP address or CIDR range and its description.
ipAllowlist.removeDescription: This will remove "{{ip}}" from the allowlist. Requests from this IP will be blocked if other IPs are still configured.
settings.title: Workspace settings
settings.nameTooLong: Name must be {{max}} characters or less.
settings.slugTooLong: Slug must be {{max}} characters or less.
settings.slugUnique: Used in URLs and the AppKit boot response.
encryption.title: Encryption Keys
encryption.intro: If you want to keep all your secrets with your Config Keys in one place, set up Encryption Keys. Secrets are encrypted in the browser before saving, so ManyRows never receives plaintext. Your server keeps the private key and decrypts secrets at runtime.
encryption.trustModel: Trust model: ManyRows stores your public key. Your team keeps the private key on your server to decrypt secrets at runtime.
encryption.setupDescription: Complete the steps in order. This ensures you never lose the private key.
encryption.step1.label: Generate key pair
encryption.step1.description: Generate an ECDH (P-256) key pair locally in your browser. ManyRows never receives the private key.
encryption.step2.label: Copy private key
encryption.step2.description: Copy the private key JSON and store it on your server (e.g. a secret manager). ManyRows cannot recover it later.
encryption.step2.required: Required step: copy the private key first. ManyRows never stores it.
encryption.step3.label: Save public key
encryption.step3.description: Save only the public key to ManyRows. This enables browser-side encryption for all secrets in this workspace.
encryption.step3.active: This workspace has an active encryption key configured.
encryption.complete.title: All steps completed
encryption.complete.description: Encryption keys are set up for this workspace. Store your private key securely on your server.
encryption.rotateWarning: Rotating the key means new secrets will be encrypted with the new public key. Ensure your server has the matching private key before rotating.
encryption.dialog.title: Private key (store this on your server)
encryption.dialog.warning: This private key is generated in your browser. ManyRows will never store it. Copy it now and save it in your server's secret manager. If you lose it, encrypted secrets cannot be recovered.
encryption.dialog.metadataNote: You do not need this metadata to decrypt secrets. It's only for confirming which key you saved.
encryption.dialog.privateKeyNote: Copy and store this JSON. This is the only data required to decrypt secrets.
encryption.snackbar.copied: Private key copied. Next: save the public key.
encryption.snackbar.clipboardBlocked: Clipboard blocked. Select the text and copy manually.
encryption.sdk.title: Decrypting secrets in your server
encryption.sdk.description: Use your private key to decrypt secrets at runtime. The Go module is `go get`-able; the Node, Python, and Java sources are on GitHub - copy the file into your project, or vendor the repo directly.
encryption.sdk.schemeDescription: Secrets are returned as JSON envelopes containing the ephemeral public key, IV, and ciphertext.
encryption.sdk.helpersNote: Each helper takes the raw envelope (object or JSON string) and your workspace private JWK, and returns the JSON-encoded plaintext.
encryption.sdk.securityReminder: Never expose your private key in client-side code or logs. Store it securely in your server's secret manager (e.g. AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, or an environment variable).
validate.title: Validate email
validate.heading: One-time email verification
validate.description: We'll email a one-time verification code to confirm you own this address.
validate.codeHelper: Enter the 6-digit code from your email.
validate.sendPrompt: Click "Send code" to receive a verification code. You only need to do this once.
validate.error.invalidCode: Invalid code or expired. Please request a new code.
validate.error.tooManyAttempts: Too many attempts. Please wait and try again.

permissions.description: Define permission slugs used by roles and access checks.
permissions.noPermissionsDesc: Permissions are the building blocks of access control. Define granular actions like
permissions.tip: Tip: Keep slugs stable - changing them can break permission checks in your UI and backend.

permissions.dialog.deleteWarningGeneric: This will delete this permission. Any roles using it may need to be updated.
roles.title: Roles
roles.description: Define roles and assign permissions. Roles can be attached to users or API keys.
roles.tip: Click a role to edit permissions. Changes are saved automatically.

roles.limitReachedWithCount: Role limit reached ({{count}}/{{max}}). Upgrade to add more roles.
roles.editDisabled: Editing disabled due to plan limits.
roles.tipFinal: Tip: Roles are used when assigning members to products. Keep slugs stable.
roles.emptyState.title: No roles yet
roles.emptyState.description: Roles define what users can do in your product. Create roles like Admin, Editor, or Viewer, then assign permissions to each role.
roles.permDialog.title: Permissions - {{name}}
roles.permDialog.subtitle: Select what this role can do.
roles.permDialog.editDisabled: Editing is disabled due to plan limits.
roles.permDialog.info: Tick permissions to grant them to this role. Unticked permissions are not granted.
roles.permDialog.saveNote: Changes apply only after you click Save.
roles.permDialog.noPermissions: No permissions exist yet. Create permissions first before assigning them to roles.
roles.dupDialog.title: Duplicate role
roles.dupDialog.description: This will create a copy of {{name}} with all its permissions.
roles.dupDialog.descriptionFallback: This will create a copy of this role with all its permissions.
roles.dupDialog.renameHint: You can rename the role after it's created.
roles.delDialog.title: Delete role
roles.delDialog.editDisabled: Editing is disabled due to plan limits.
roles.delDialog.warning: You're about to delete {{name}}. This cannot be undone.
roles.delDialog.warningFallback: You're about to delete this role. This cannot be undone.
roles.delDialog.memberNote: Members assigned this role will lose its permissions. If your backend enforces referential integrity, you may need to remove member-role assignments first.
projectMembers.title: Users
projectMembers.reset2faBody: The user's two-factor authentication (authenticator app) will be removed. They'll sign in without a second factor until they set it up again — use this when a user has lost their authenticator.
projectMembers.unlockBody: Clears a failed-login lockout so the user can sign in again immediately.
projectMembers.createAppFirst: Create an app before adding users
projectMembers.createRoleFirst: Create a role before adding users
projectMembers.noAppDesc: Create an app before managing user access.
projectMembers.selectApp: Select an app above to view and manage user access.
projectMembers.noRolesDesc: Create a role before adding users to this app.

projectMembers.dialog.noRolesWarning: No roles exist yet. Create a role first, then you can assign it.
projectMembers.dialog.selectRoleWarning: Select at least one role to save.
projectMembers.dialog.noRolesInfo: Saving with no roles selected will remove all roles for this user in {{app}}.
apps.defaultRoleRequired: Default role is required when registration is enabled.
apps.dialog.deleteWarning: Are you sure you want to delete this app? This action cannot be undone.
apps.dialog.enabledDesc: Disabled apps cannot be accessed via the API or AppKit SDK.
apps.dialog.allowedDomainsHelper: Comma-separated list of allowed domains. Leave empty to allow all domains.

features.title: Feature Flags
features.unlimitedFlags: Unlimited feature flags on this plan
features.scope.clientHelp: Can be delivered to frontend (NOT secure) and backend SDKs
features.info.title: Feature flags are for fast on/off control.
features.info.description: Use them to enable/disable features instantly per app, without redeploying. Flags are defined on the product (shared across all apps); each app toggles its own value per app. If you need values beyond a boolean (strings, numbers, JSON, lists), use Config Keys instead.
features.info.scope: Scope controls where the flag can be delivered: Server is backend-only; Client may be delivered to frontend (not secure).
features.overLimitWarning: You are over your plan limit for feature flags ({{current}} / {{limit}}). Editing is disabled until you upgrade or reduce usage.
features.atLimitWarning: You have reached your plan limit for feature flags ({{current}} / {{limit}}). You can edit existing flags, but you can't create new ones until you upgrade.
features.noApps: No apps found for this product. Create apps first, then you can toggle flags per app.
features.selectApp: Select an app to view and edit values.
features.empty.title: No Feature Flags Yet
features.empty.description: Feature flags let you enable or disable features instantly without redeploying. Great for gradual rollouts, A/B testing, or emergency kill switches.
features.empty.tip1: Create a flag with a unique key (e.g., new_checkout_flow)
features.empty.tip2: Toggle it on/off per app (dev, staging, prod)
features.empty.tip3: Use the SDK to check the flag in your code
features.killSwitchTooltip: Kill switch: disable in ALL apps
features.prodConfirm.title: Are you sure?
features.prodConfirm.description: You're about to switch to {{app}}. This is production.
features.prodConfirm.warning: Changes you make here may affect live traffic. Double-check before saving.
features.dialog.overLimitCreate: You are over your plan limit ({{current}} / {{limit}}). You can't create new flags until you upgrade or delete some.
features.dialog.atLimitCreate: You have reached your plan limit ({{current}} / {{limit}}). Upgrade to add more feature flags.
features.dialog.overLimitEdit: You are over your plan limit ({{current}} / {{limit}}). Editing is disabled until you upgrade or delete some flags.
features.dialog.keyHelper: Lowercase letters, numbers, underscore. Used by SDKs.
features.dialog.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete {{key}}?
features.dialog.killSwitchWarning: This will immediately disable the feature flag in ALL {{count}} apps, including production.
features.dialog.killSwitchHelp: Use this for emergencies when a feature is causing issues. You can re-enable the flag later.
features.dialog.enableAllWarning: This will immediately enable the feature flag in ALL {{count}} apps, including production.
features.dialog.enableAllHelp: This will make the feature available in all apps immediately. You can disable it per-app later.
features.snackbar.limitReached: Plan limit reached ({{count}}/{{max}}). Upgrade to add more feature flags.
features.snackbar.overLimitEdit: Feature flags are over the plan limit ({{count}}/{{max}}). Editing is disabled until you upgrade or reduce usage.
features.snackbar.editDisabled: Editing is disabled due to plan limits.
features.snackbar.killSwitchActivated: Kill switch activated: "{{key}}" disabled in all apps
configKeys.title: Config Keys
configKeys.overLimitWarning: Over plan limit: {{count}} / {{max}}. Delete keys to enable editing.
configKeys.atLimitWarning: Plan limit reached: {{count}} / {{max}}. Delete a key to add more.
configKeys.empty.title: No config keys yet
configKeys.empty.description: Config keys let you manage per-app settings like API URLs, feature limits, and secrets.
configKeys.selectEnvToEdit: Select an app above to edit values
configKeys.secretsRequireKey: Secrets require a workspace encryption key. Go to Security → Encryption Keys.
configKeys.exposure.publicDesc: Public values are safe to expose to client-side apps. Use for non-sensitive settings.
configKeys.exposure.privateDesc: Private values are server-only. They should never be exposed to client-side apps.
configKeys.exposure.secretDesc: Secret values are encrypted in the browser with your workspace public key and are write-only in the admin UI. You can set/rotate them, but they will never be shown again here. Your backend decrypts them at runtime using the matching private key - see the Encryption Key tab for the per-language SDK helpers.
configKeys.exposure.defaultDesc: Exposure controls where this value may be used and whether it can be read back.
configKeys.writeOnlyHelper: Write-only. Enter a new value to set/rotate.
configKeys.secretsRequireKeyShort: Secrets require a workspace encryption key.
configKeys.about.title: Config Keys are per-app settings (API URLs, limits, etc.). Keys are defined on the product (name, type, exposure - shared across all apps); values are set per app - that's what you're editing here.
configKeys.about.exposureControls: Exposure controls where values can be used:
configKeys.about.secretEncrypted: Encrypted in the browser, decrypted on your backend with our SDK (write-only here)
configKeys.about.secretsRequire: Secrets require a workspace encryption key. Open the Encryption Key tab to set one up.
configKeys.exposureInfo.public: Public values can be exposed to client-side apps. Use for non-sensitive config like UI labels, feature text, or public keys intended for the browser.
configKeys.exposureInfo.secret: Secret values are encrypted in the browser with your workspace public key and are write-only in the admin UI - you can set or rotate them, but they will never be shown again here. To use a secret, your backend pulls it from the delivery API and decrypts it at runtime with the matching private key. The Encryption Key tab links to the Go / Node / Python / Java decrypt helpers.
configKeys.exposureInfo.secretSetup: Setup required: Configure a workspace encryption key first (open the Encryption Key tab). Without a public key, ManyRows can't accept encrypted secret values.
configKeys.exposureInfo.private: Private values are server-only. They must never be exposed to client-side apps. Use for internal settings that aren't secrets but should stay backend-only.
configKeys.prodConfirm.title: Are you sure?
configKeys.prodConfirm.description: You're about to switch to {{app}}. This is production.
configKeys.prodConfirm.warning: Double-check what you're changing before saving. This can affect real users.
configKeys.dialog.limitReached: - limit reached. Delete a key to add another.
configKeys.dialog.keyHelper: Allowed: letters, numbers, underscore, dash, dot
configKeys.dialog.deleteDescription: This will delete the config key and all its app values.
configKeys.snackbar.limitReached: Config key limit reached ({{count}}/{{max}}). Delete a key to add another.
configKeys.snackbar.invalidKey: Key must be non-empty and use only letters, numbers, _, -, .
configKeys.snackbar.encryptionKeyRequire: Workspace encryption key not configured (Security → Encryption Keys)
workspaceHome.accessRestrictedDesc: Only owners and admins can view or edit workspace settings. Ask an owner or admin to grant you access.
webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.description: Manage webhook endpoints that receive event notifications.
webhooks.deleteConfirmation: Are you sure you want to delete this webhook? All delivery history will also be deleted.
webhooks.secretWarning: Copy this secret now. You won't be able to see it again.
webhooks.noFilteredDeliveries: No deliveries match the selected filter.
webhooks.maxWebhooks: This app already has a webhook configured.
sessions.pruneConfirmDescription: This will permanently delete all expired sessions for this workspace. Active sessions are not affected.
sessions.pruneNone: No expired sessions to clean up

wizard.project.slugHelper: Used in URLs and APIs. Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.
